narrative.headline: 2022 Jeep Renegade: electrical system tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 23 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 48%, followed by the engine (35%) and the power train (26%). No crashes, fires, injuries, or deaths are reported across any area.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Jeep Renegade. The contact stated that while her daughter was driving in traffic, several warning lights appeared on the instrument panel as the vehicle stalled. The contact's daughter called the police who assisted her daughter with moving the vehicle off
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 11 reports (48%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for warning lights appearing on the dashboard.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 8 reports (35%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for engine shudder or trouble accelerating.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 6 reports (26%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Listen for a loud bang from underneath during acceleration or deceleration.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 4 reports (17%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for the front collision warning light coming on with no apparent cause.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The fewest reports: 3 (13%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A smaller complaint area tied to fuel and power delivery.
